*�����������������"# me is this DAT.
#
# comp is the List Component that holds this panel.
# row is the row number of the cell being updated.
# col is the column number of the cell being updated.
#
# attribs contains the following members:
#
# text				   str			cell contents.
# help				 str			 help text
#
# textColor			r g b a		font color
# textOffsetX		   n			  horizontal text offset
# textOffsetY		   n			  vertical text offset
# textJustify		   m			  m is one of:  JustifyType.TOPLEFT, JustifyType.TOPCENTER,
#													JustifyType.TOPRIGHT, JustifyType.CENTERLEFT,
#													JustifyType.CENTER, JustifyType.CENTERRIGHT,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMLEFT, JustifyType.BOTTOMCENTER,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMRIGHT
#
# bgColor			  r g b a		background color
#
# leftBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside left border color
# rightBorderInColor   r g b a		  inside right border color
# topBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside top border color
# bottomBorderInColor  r g b a		  inside bottom border color
#
# leftBorderOutColor   r g b a		  outside left border color
# rightBorderOutColor  r g b a		  outside right border colorh
# topBorderOutColor	   r g b a		  outside top border color
# bottomBorderOutColor r g b a		  outside bottom border color
#
# colWidth			 w			  sets column width
# colStetch			True/False	 sets column stretchiness (width is min width)
# rowHeight			h			  sets row height
#
# editable			   bool			  contents are editable
# fontBold			 True/False	 render font bolded
# fontItalic		   True/False	 render font italicized
# fontSizeX			float		  font X size in pixels
# fontSizeX			float		  font Y size in pixels, if not specified, uses X size
# fontFace			 str			  font face, example 'Verdana'
# wordWrap			 True/False	 word wrap
#
# image				TOP			  background TOP operator
#
# select   true when the cell/row/col is currently being selected by the mouse.
# rollover true when the mouse is currently over the cell/row/col.
# radio	true when the cell/row/col was last selected.
# focus	true when the cell/row/col is being edited.
#
# currently not implemented:
#
# type				str			 cell type: 'field' or 'label'
# fieldtype		   str			 field type: 'float' 'string' or  'integer'
# setpos			  True/False x y  set cell absolute when first argument is True
# padding			 l r b t		 cell padding from each edge, expressed in pixels
# margin			  l r b t		 cell margin from neighbouring cells, expressed in pixels
#
# fontpath			path			File location to font. Don't use with 'font'
# fontformat		  str			 font format: 'polygon', 'outline' or 'bitmap'
# fontantialiased	 True/False	  render font antialiased
# fontcharset		 str			 font character set
#
# textjustify		 h v			 left/right/center top/center/bottom
# textoffset		  x y			 text position offset
#

#called when Reset parameter is pulsed, or on load

def onInitCell(comp, row, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitRow(comp, row, attribs):
	return


def onInitCol(comp, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitTable(comp, attribs):
	try:
		listerExt = ext.ListerExt
	except:
		pass
	else:
		listerExt.onInitTable(attribs)

	return

#called during specific events
#
# coords is a named tuple containing the following members:
#   x
#   y
#   u
#   v


def onRollover(comp, row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol, prevcoords):
	# debug('rollover', row, col)
	ext.ListerExt.onRollover(row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol,
										prevcoords)
	return


def onSelect(comp, startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow, endcol, endcoords,
			 start, end):
	# print(me, 'onSelect', comp, startrow, start, end)
	ext.ListerExt.onSelect(startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow,
										endcol, endcoords, start, end)
	return


def onRadio(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	# print(onRadio, comp, row, col)
	return


def onFocus(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	ext.ListerExt.onFocus( row, col, prevrow, prevcol)
	return

def onEdit(comp, row, col, val):
	# print("onEdit", row, col, val)
	ext.ListerExt.onEdit( row, col, val)
	return

# return True if interested in this drop event, False otherwise
def onHover(comp, row, col, coords,	prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		retVal = ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(True, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)
		return retVal

def onDrop(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		return ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(False, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)

*�����������������{# me - this DAT.
# 
# dat - the changed DAT
# rows - a list of row indices
# cols - a list of column indices
# cells - the list of cells that have changed content
# prev - the list of previous string contents of the changed cells
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the DAT Execute DAT.
# 
# If rows or columns are deleted, sizeChange will be called instead of row/col/cellChange.


def tableChange(dat):
	if dat.numCols != ext.ListerExt.nCols and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval():
		ext.ListerExt.setupAutoColDefine(True)

	parent.Lister.Refresh()
	return

def rowChange(dat, rows):
	if 0 in rows and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval() and parent.Lister.par.Header.eval():
		run('args[0].setupAutoColDefine(True)',ext.ListerExt,delayFrames=1)
	return

def colChange(dat, cols):
	return

def cellChange(dat, cells, prev):
	return

def sizeChange(dat):
	return

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urljoin
from urllib.request import urlopen
from urllib.error import HTTPError

import jsonschema
import TDFunctions as TDF
from jsonschema import RefResolver, validate
from packaging import version
from TDStoreTools import StorageManager

class SignalingClientExt:
	"""	
	The signalingClient COMP is a component that can be used to connect to a
	signalingServer COMP within TouchDesigner as well as exchange messages with
	other signalingClients through the signaling server it is connected with.

	When a client is sending a signaling message to the server, a target should
	be specified as part of the Signaling API. More details about the Signaling
	API are available at Palette:signalingServer#SignalingAPI

	The server is using the target property to route the message to a matching,
	connected, client.

	The receiving client is then parsing the message and routing the message to
	a matching method using the type property.

	i.e. A signalingClient COMP is receiving a message where 
	message['signalingType'] is equal to ClientEntered,
	the onMessageReceivedClientEntered() method will be called.

	If no matching method is found, the signalingClient will fail gracefully.

	While we advise to make use of Subscribers and the Subscribe() method to
	extend the signalingClient, users that want to extend the Signaling API with
	custom types should add new methods prefixed by onMessageReceived.

	i.e If a user wants to add a custom type Animal, a matching method
	onMessageReceivedAnimal() should be available in the
	Palette:signalingClient Ext.

	More details about Subscribers and the Subscribe() method are available on
	this page.

	The communication protocol used as part of this signalingClient is
	WebSocket.

	It is often used as part of WebRTC sessions.
	"""
	class Client:
		"""
		The Client class describes a client object as part of the signaling
		session.
		"""
		def __init__(self, **kwargs) -> None:
			"""
			The id member is generated as a UUID by the server when a new client
			is connecting.

			The address member is set by the server when a new client is
			connecting. It is the IP address and the port used by the client to
			establish the WebSocket handshake in IP:port format and relative to
			the server.

			The properties member can be used to hold an arbitrary dictionary
			and extend the Signaling API with custom data. By default {'domain':
			'/'}
			"""
			self.id = tdu.Dependency(kwargs.get('id', None))
			self.address = tdu.Dependency(kwargs.get('address', ''))
			self.properties = tdu.Dependency(kwargs.get('properties', {'domain': '/'}))
			
			if parent.SignalingClient.extensionsReady and not kwargs.get('asClient', None):
				parent.SignalingClient.CurrentClients.appendRow([self.id.val, self.address.val, self.properties.val])
			
			elif not kwargs.get('asClient', None):
				op('currentClients').appendRow([self.id.val, self.address.val, self.properties.val])

			pass

		def __del__(self) -> None:
			if hasattr(parent, 'SignalingClient') and parent.SignalingClient.extensionsReady and hasattr(parent.SignalingClient, 'Logger'):
				parent.SignalingClient.Logger.Debug(f'Client {self.id.val} w/ address {self.address.val} was destroyed.')
			
			return

		def AsJSON(self) -> dict:
			"""
			A utility method to convert a Client object to a dict / JSON
			friendly object.

			Returns:
				dict: A client object as a dict
			"""			
			asJSON = {}
			asJSON['id'] = self.id.val
			asJSON['address'] = self.address.val
			asJSON['properties'] = self.properties.val
			return asJSON

	"""
	SignalingClientExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		"""
		The SignalingClientExt constructor drives the signalingClient COMP and
		exposes various promoted methods in addition to holding data
		during a signaling session, including subscribers.

		Subscribers are other TouchDesigner components that might rely on the
		signalingClient COMP and Signaling API.

		You can add Subscribers by calling the method Subscribe() and passing a
		component as well as an array of strings where this array of strings represents
		message types to route to the subscriber whenever the signalingClient
		receives a message with a matching type from the signaling server.

		In a similar fashion to routing within the signalingClient COMP, a
		subscriber should implement a method prefixed with onMessageReceived and
		followed by the message type.

		i.e. We have a WebRTC COMP calling Subscribe() and passing itself, as
		well as a string array such as ['Offer', 'Answer']. If the
		signalingClient COMP is receiving a message of type Offer it will call
		the method onMessageReceivedOffer() on the Subscriber (the WebRTC COMP
		in this specific case).

		Args:
			ownerComp (COMP): The signalingClient COMP to which this extension
			is attached.
		"""
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.Logger = op('logger')

		"""
		Point to a Client instance that describes the signalingClient COMP as if
		it was a Client. The Client class describes a client object as part of
		the signaling session.
		"""
		TDF.createProperty(
			self,
			'AsClient',
			value=self.Client(asClient=True),
			dependable=True,
			readOnly=False
		)

		self.messagesMetadata = {
			'apiVersion': self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval(),
			'compVersion': self.ownerComp.par.Version.eval(),
			'compOrigin': self.ownerComp.path,
			'projectName': project.name,
		}

		self.ws = op('websocket1')
		self.ws.par.reset.pulse()

		self.signalingSchemaStore = {}
		self.signalingSchemaRefResolver = {}
		self.signalingSchemaValidators = {}

		self.signalingCheckTimer = op('timer1')
		
		"""
		A reference to a DAT, copy of the Clients member.
		"""
		self.CurrentClients = op('currentClients')
		self.CurrentClients.clear(keepFirstRow=True)

		"""
		A list of Client objects that are currently in the same signaling
		session as the signalingClient. It includes all other clients in the
		same session and on the same domain other than itself.
		"""
		TDF.createProperty(
			self,
			'Clients',
			value=[],
			dependable=True,
			readOnly=False
		)

		"""
		A list of components in the current TouchDesigner project that subscribed to
		the signalingClient COMP using the Subscribe() method.

		Each of those list items are dictionaries such as:

		{
			'origin': 'path/to/a/COMP',
			'messageTypes':['a','string','array','of','types']
		}
		"""
		TDF.createProperty(
			self,
			'Subscribers',
			value=[],
			dependable='deep',
			readOnly=False
		)
				
		run(
			"args[0].postInit() if args[0] "
					"and hasattr(args[0], 'postInit') else None",
			self,
			endFrame=True,
			delayRef=op.TDResources
		)

	def postInit(self):
		self.signalingCheck()
		self.signalingCheckTimer.par.start.pulse()

		if self.ownerComp.par.Uselocalrefresolver:
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Parameter Folder change was triggered.')
			self.getSchemaStore(self.ownerComp.par.Localschemafolder.eval())
		return

	def __delTD__(self):
		self.Clients = []
		self.selfRef = None
		self.Logger.Debug('Signaling Client and Clients references were removed.')	

	"""
	Signaling, receiver and handlers
	"""
	def onReceiveText(self, data: str) -> None:
		"""
		This is a direct callback used when a client received a message from a
		server or other client.  

		The incoming message metadata dictionary is being used to confirm that
		the message comply with the Signaling API and that the message format is
		using a compatible Signaling API version before sending to the router
		onMessageReceived().

		Optionally, using the SignalingClient parameters related to incoming
		JSON Schema validation of messages will trigger validation on any
		messages received with a type available in the schema repository. 

		Args:
			data (str): The message received by the client from the server, as a
			JSON string, made of the mandatory properties metadata, signalingType, sender
			and content.
		"""
		dataAsJSONDict = json.loads(data)

		if self.ownerComp.par.Usejsonschemain and not self.validateWithSchema(dataAsJSONDict):
			return

		# The following JSON properties are informations about the Signaling
		# protocol and are mandatory
		metadata = dataAsJSONDict.get('metadata', None)
				
		if not metadata:
			self.Logger.Error(f'The metadata could not be found. Invalid signaling format message. Aborting')
			return

		apiVersion = metadata.get('apiVersion', None)
		compVersion = metadata.get('compVersion', None)
		compOrigin = metadata.get('compOrigin', None)
		projectName = metadata.get('projectName', None)
		
		sender = dataAsJSONDict.get('sender', None)
		
		if not self.isVersionMatching(apiVersion):
			return
		else:
			self.Logger.Debug(f'Message received from {sender}:{projectName}:{compOrigin}')
		
		self.onMessageReceived(dataAsJSONDict)

	def onReceiveBinary(self, contents: bytearray) -> None:
		"""
		Any binary messages should be implemented additionally by the user as
		custom code

		Args:
			contents (bytearray): A byte array of the message contents.
		"""
		multiLineString = f'''
			OnReceiveBinary is not yet implemented and should be implented by
			the user as custom code.  The following byte array will be ignored
			{contents}
			'''
		self.Logger.Info(multiLineString)

		return

	def onReceivePing(self, contents: bytearray) -> None:
		"""
		This is a direct callback used when a client received a ping from the
		server.

		Args:
			contents (bytearray): A byte array of the message contents.
		"""
		self.ws.sendPong(contents)
		return
	
	def onReceivePong(self, contents: bytearray) -> None:
		"""
		This is a direct callback used when a server sent a pong back to the
		client following a ping.

		Args:
			contents (bytearray): A byte array of the message contents.
		"""
		self.Logger.Info(f'onReceivePong: {contents}')
		return
	
	def onMonitorMessage(self, message: str) -> None:
		"""
		This is a direct callback used when the WebSocket connection change
		status.

		Args:
			message (str): A unicode representation of the message.
		"""
		self.Logger.Info(f'onMonitorMessage: {message}')
		return

	def onMessageReceived(self, dataAsJSONDict: dict) -> None:
		"""
		This is a router used to route messages to matching methods based on
		type as described in the SignalingClientExt class description.

		This router will route messages to any Subscribers when a Subscriber
		registered to the SignalingClient COMP using the Subscribe() method.

		It will fail gracefully when no matching method is found in the
		SignalingClient.
		
		While it is likely that additional message types would be implemented in
		a Subscriber, users that want to extend the Signaling API with custom
		types should add new methods with prefix onMessageReceived.  i.e if a
		user wants to add a custom type 'Banana', a matching method
		onMessageReceivedBanana() should be available in the SignalingClientExt.

		Args:
			dataAsJSONDict (dict): The message received by the server from the
			client, as a JSON dictionary, made of the mandatory properties
			metadata, type, sender and content.
		"""
		signalingType = dataAsJSONDict.get('signalingType', '')

		isTypeUnknown = True
		if hasattr(self, 'onMessageReceived' + signalingType):
			getattr(self, 'onMessageReceived' + signalingType)(dataAsJSONDict)
			isTypeUnknown = False

		for subscriber in self.Subscribers:
			if signalingType in subscriber['messageTypes']:
				if hasattr(subscriber['origin'], 'OnMessageReceived'):
					getattr(subscriber['origin'], 'OnMessageReceived')(dataAsJSONDict)
					isTypeUnknown = False
				else:
					self.Logger.Warning(f'OnMessageReceived is not implemented in subscriber.')

		if isTypeUnknown:
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Signaling type {signalingType} appears to be unknown or no method onMessageReceived{signalingType} was associated with it in the SignalingClient or any Subscriber.')

		return
	
	def onMessageReceivedClientEntered(self, dataAsJSONDict: dict) -> None:
		"""
		This method is called when this client successfully connected to the
		server.  It will parse the data found in the content property, and
		update this client id and address as identified by the server.

		Args:
			dataAsJSONDict (dict): The message received by the server from the
			client, as a JSON dictionary, made of the mandatory properties
			metadata, type, sender and content.
				content: A dictionary made of the property 'self'
					self: A dictionary made of the properties 'id' and
					'address'
		"""
		content = dataAsJSONDict.get('content', None)

		if not content:
			self.Logger.Error(f'Content property was missing from message. Aborting.')
			return
		
		self.AsClient.id.val = content['self']['id']
		self.AsClient.address.val = content['self']['address']
		self.AsClient.properties.val = content['self']['properties']

	def onMessageReceivedClientEnter(self, dataAsJSONDict: dict) -> None:
		"""
		This method is called when another client entered the signaling session.
		It will parse the data found in the content property, and create a new
		client (other than self) on the signaling client end for later exchange
		of messages (to be used as a target).

		Args:
			dataAsJSONDict (dict): The message received by the server from the
			client, as a JSON dictionary, made of the mandatory properties
			metadata, type, sender and content.
				content: A dictionary made of the property 'client'
					client: A dictionary made of the properties 'id' and
					'address'
		"""
		client = self.GetClientById(dataAsJSONDict['content']['client']['id'])

		if not client:
			newClient = self.Client(**dataAsJSONDict['content']['client'])
			self.Clients.append(newClient)

		return

	def onMessageReceivedClientExit(self, dataAsJSONDict: dict) -> None:
		"""
		This method is called when another client left the signaling session.
		It will parse the data found in the content property, and remove the
		existing client (other than self) on the signaling client end.

		Args:
			dataAsJSONDict (dict): The message received by the server from the
			client, as a JSON dictionary, made of the mandatory properties
			metadata, type, sender and content.
				content: A dictionary made of the property 'id'
		"""
		client = self.GetClientById(dataAsJSONDict['content']['client']['id'])

		# Using ID from dict here, the client object might already be destroyed
		if self.CurrentClients.row(dataAsJSONDict['content']['client']['id']):
			self.CurrentClients.deleteRow(dataAsJSONDict['content']['client']['id'])
		
		newClientsList = [client_ for client_ in self.Clients if dataAsJSONDict['content']['client']['id'] != client_.id.val]
		self.Clients = newClientsList
		return

	def onMessageReceivedClients(self, dataAsJSONDict: dict) -> None:
		"""
		This method is called when this client successfully connected to the
		server. 

		It will parse the data found in the content property, which is an array
		of dictionary that represents clients already in the Signaling Session.
		Or an empty array if no other client was present in the Session.

		Args:
			dataAsJSONDict (dict): The message received by the server from the
			client, as a JSON dictionary, made of the mandatory properties
			metadata, type, sender and content.
				content: A dictionary made of the property 'clients', an array
				of clients
					client: A dictionary made of the properties 'id', 'address'
		"""
		if len(dataAsJSONDict['content']['clients']):
			clientsCurrentlyOnSignalingCOMP = {client.address.val for client in self.Clients}
			clientsFromServer = {client['address'] for client in dataAsJSONDict['content']['clients']}

			for address in clientsCurrentlyOnSignalingCOMP - clientsFromServer:

				client = self.GetClientByAddress(address)

				# Using ID from dict here, the client object might already be destroyed
				if self.CurrentClients.row(client.id.val):
					self.CurrentClients.deleteRow(client.id.val)
				
				self.Clients.remove(client)

			missingClientsAsDict = [client for client in dataAsJSONDict['content']['clients'] if client['address'] in clientsFromServer - clientsCurrentlyOnSignalingCOMP]
			for clientAsDict in missingClientsAsDict:
				newClient = self.Client(**clientAsDict)
				self.Clients.append(newClient)				

		else:
			# They are no clients connected to the server, this client should clear it's
			# client list.
			self.Clients = []
			self.CurrentClients.clear(keepFirstRow=True)
		
		return

	"""
	Signaling, sender
	"""
	def Send(self, dataAsJSONDict: dict) -> None:
		"""
		This method converts a dictionary to a JSON string and sends it as text
		to the signaling server.

		This method can be used by Subscribers or other external components to send
		their own messages to the signaling server by leveraging the
		signalingClient COMP.

		Args:
			dataAsJSONDict (dict): A dictionary, following the Signaling API
			format, with mandatory properties.
		"""
		if self.ownerComp.par.Usejsonschemaout and not self.validateWithSchema(dataAsJSONDict):
			return
				
		self.ws.sendText(json.dumps(dataAsJSONDict))
		return

	def SendBinary(self, dataAsByteArray: bytearray) -> None:
		"""
		This method can be used by Subscribers or other external components to send
		their own messages as byte array to the signaling server by leveraging
		the signalingClient COMP.
		"""
		self.ws.sendBinary(dataAsByteArray)
		return
	
	"""
	Par Callbacks
	"""
	def onUselocalrefresolverChange(self, par):
		if par.eval():
			self.signalingSchemaStore = {}
			self.signalingSchemaRefResolver = {}
			self.signalingSchemaValidators = {}
			self.getSchemaStore(self.ownerComp.par.Localschemafolder.eval())
		else:
			self.signalingSchemaStore = {}
		
		return

	def onLocalschemafolderChange(self, par):
		if par.eval():
			self.signalingSchemaStore = {}
			self.signalingSchemaRefResolver = {}
			self.signalingSchemaValidators = {}
			self.getSchemaStore(self.ownerComp.par.Localschemafolder.eval())
		else:
			self.signalingSchemaStore = {}
				
		return
	
	def onClearschemacachePulse(self, par):
		self.signalingSchemaStore = {}
		self.signalingSchemaRefResolver = {}
		self.signalingSchemaValidators = {}

	"""
		Utilities and helpers
	"""
	def Subscribe(self, externalComp: str, messageTypes: List[str]) -> bool:
		"""
		This method is used to add an external component as a Subscriber of this
		signalingClient COMP.

		When a component is registered as a Subscriber, any message type received by
		the signalingClient that are also in the message types list that the
		Subscriber registered for will be routed to the Subscriber.

		Args:
			externalComp (str): The path to a COMP that is registering as a
			Subscriber on this signaling client.
			messageTypes (List[str]): A list of message types that the 
			Subscriber is registering for. When the signaling client is 
			receiving a matching message type, it will route it to 
			the Subscriber.

		Returns:
			bool: Whether the subscription request was a success or not. Will
			return False if the Subscriber was already registered.
		"""
		for subscriber in self.Subscribers:
			if subscriber['origin'] == externalComp:
				subscriber['messageTypes'] = messageTypes
				self.Logger.Info(f'Subscriber candidate is already in list of known subscribers, updating message types.')
				return False
		
		if not externalComp.isCOMP:
			self.Logger.Error('The subscriber is not of type COMP.')

		self.Subscribers.append({'origin': externalComp, 'messageTypes': messageTypes})
		self.Logger.Info(f'{externalComp} subscribed to signaling messages types {messageTypes}')
		
		return True

	def GetClientsAsJSON(self):
		"""
		A utility method that will return a list of clients as JSON / dicts.

		Returns:
			List[dict]: A list of clients as JSON / dicts.
		"""
		clients = []
		for client in self.Clients:
			clients.append(client.AsJSON())
		return clients

	def GetClientByAddress(self, address: str) -> Optional[Client]:
		"""
		A utility method that will return a client object provided an IP:port
		address.

		Args:
			address (str): An IP:port address string.

		Returns:
			Client: A client object with a matching IP:port.
		"""
		for client in self.Clients:
			if client.address.val == address:
				return client

		return None

	def GetClientById(self, id: str) -> Optional[Client]:
		"""
		A utility method that will return a client object provided an ID.

		Args:
			id (str): A UUID as a string.

		Returns:
			Client: A client object with a matching UUID.
		"""
		for client in self.Clients:
			if client.id.val == id:
				return client

		return None

	def signalingCheck(self) -> None:
		"""
		A utility method to verify whether this signaling client is currently
		connected to a signaling server.
		
		Attempts to reconnect to the signaling server when disconnected.
		"""
		if not eval(self.ownerComp.op('wsState')[1, 'connected'].val):
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Client is not connected to server, attempting to reconnect.')
			self.ws.par.reset.pulse()

	def Reset(self) -> None:
		"""
		Reset the Signaling session (on the client's end) and all states of the
		signalingClient COMP and its SignalingClientExt. 
		
		Attempts to reconnect to the signaling server if a signaling server IP
		was specified as a parameter and if the signaling server is online.
		"""
		self.Clients = []
		self.CurrentClients.clear(keepFirstRow=True)
		self.Subscribers.val = []
		self.ws.par.reset.pulse()
		self.signalingCheck()
		self.signalingCheckTimer.par.start.pulse()

	def isVersionMatching(self, apiVersion: str) -> bool:
		"""
		This method is used to confirm that the Signaling API version is
		matching between clients and server.

		Args:
			apiVersion (str): A version number as a string, to compare with the
			signalingServer COMP own version.

		Returns:
			bool: A boolean that represents whether the version is matching or
			not.
		"""
		if version.parse(apiVersion) != version.parse(self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval()):
			self.Logger.Error(f'API version of client {apiVersion} does not match API version of server {self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval()}. Aborting.')
			return False

		else:
			return True

	"""
		Schema related
	"""
	def validateWithSchema(self, messageAsDict):
		valid = False

		signalingType = messageAsDict.get('signalingType', None)
		signalingType = signalingType if signalingType else 'Message'
		schemaType = signalingType if signalingType else 'Message'

		schemaPaths = self.getSchemaPaths(schemaType)
		schema = self.getSchema(schemaPaths)

		if schema:
			if schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'] not in self.signalingSchemaValidators.keys():
				if schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'] not in self.signalingSchemaRefResolver.keys():
					self.createResolver(schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'], schema)
				
				resolver = self.getResolver(schema, schemaPaths)
				self.createValidator(schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'], schema, resolver)
			
			validator = self.getValidator(schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'])

			try:
				validator.validate(messageAsDict)
				valid = True
			except jsonschema.ValidationError as e:
				self.Logger.Error(f'An error occured while validating the message. Aborting. {e}')

		else:
			self.Logger.Error(f'Schema {schemaType} could not be found. Aborting.')

		return valid

	def getSchemaPaths(self, schemaType):
		schemaFileName = schemaType + '.schema.json'
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Uselocalrefresolver.eval():
			schemaVersionPath = 'v' + self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval() + '/schemas/' if 'git' in self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapischemaendpoint.eval() else self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval() + '/'
			baseURIwVersion = self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapischemaendpoint.eval() + schemaVersionPath
			schemaURIwVersion = baseURIwVersion + schemaFileName

			schemaPaths = {
				'fileName': schemaFileName,
				'versionPath': schemaVersionPath,
				'baseURI': self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval(),
				'baseURIwVersion': baseURIwVersion,
				'schemaURIwVersion': schemaURIwVersion
			}
		else:
			schemaPaths = {
				'fileName': schemaFileName,
				'versionPath': '',
				'baseURI': schemaFileName,
				'baseURIwVersion': schemaFileName,
				'schemaURIwVersion': schemaFileName
			}

		return schemaPaths

	def getSchema(self, schemaPaths):
		if self.ownerComp.par.Uselocalrefresolver:
			if schemaPaths['fileName'] in self.signalingSchemaStore:
				schema = self.signalingSchemaStore[schemaPaths['fileName']]
			else:
				schema = self.getSchemaStore(self.ownerComp.par.Localschemafolder.eval())[schemaPaths['fileName']]
		
		elif schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'] not in self.signalingSchemaStore:
			try:
				response = urlopen(schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'])
				schema = json.loads(response.read())
				self.signalingSchemaStore[schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion']] = schema
			except HTTPError as e:
				schema = None
				self.Logger.Error(f'An error occured while retrieving the remote schema. Aborting. {e}')

		else:
			schema = self.signalingSchemaStore[schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion']]

		return schema

	def createResolver(self, schemaBaseURI, schema):
		resolver = RefResolver(schemaBaseURI, schema, store=self.signalingSchemaStore, cache_remote=True)
		self.signalingSchemaRefResolver[schemaBaseURI] = resolver

	def getResolver(self, schema, schemaPaths):
		if schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion'] in self.signalingSchemaRefResolver.keys():
			return self.signalingSchemaRefResolver[schemaPaths['schemaURIwVersion']]
		elif schemaPaths['fileName'] in self.signalingSchemaRefResolver.keys():
			return self.signalingSchemaRefResolver[schemaPaths['fileName']]
		else:
			resolver = RefResolver.from_schema(schema, store=self.signalingSchemaStore)
			self.signalingSchemaRefResolver[schemaPaths['fileName']] = resolver
			return resolver

	def createValidator(self, schemaBaseURI, schema, resolver):
		try:
			self.signalingSchemaValidators[schemaBaseURI] = jsonschema.Draft202012Validator(schema, resolver=resolver)
		except jsonschema.SchemaError as e:
			self.Logger.Error(f'An error occured while validating the schema. Aborting. {e}')

	def getValidator(self, schemaBaseURI):
		return self.signalingSchemaValidators[schemaBaseURI]

	def getSchemaStore(self, schemasFolder):
		if not os.path.isdir(schemasFolder):
			self.Logger.Error(f'The Schema folder {schemasFolder} could not be found.')

		for path in os.listdir(schemasFolder):
			fullPath = os.path.join(schemasFolder, path)
			
			if os.path.isfile(fullPath):
				
				with open(fullPath) as schema_file:
					schema = json.load(schema_file)
					
					self.signalingSchemaStore[f"{os.path.basename(fullPath)}"] = schema

			else:
				self.Logger.Error(f'The Schema file {fullPath} could not be found.')

		return self.signalingSchemaStore
